common.requiredFields: Please fill in all required fields

hero.title: Build Your Enterprise AI Memory Hub
hero.subtitle: Connect every knowledge point, build a growable enterprise knowledge graph. Let AI truly understand your business.

blackboard.title: Blackboard
blackboard.pageDescription: A central command layer for your current workspace: shared goals, task execution, discussions, notes, and team topology in one place.
blackboard.canvasHint: Use the central blackboard to review execution, align the team, and jump into shared tasks and discussions.
blackboard.quickActionsHint: The center tracks completion, discussions, and agent activity. Open it to edit tasks, reply to posts, and inspect topology.
blackboard.modalSubtitle: Shared goals, tasks, discussions, and topology for the active workspace.
blackboard.noPostsDescription: Create the first post to start the discussion.
blackboard.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this post?
blackboard.deleteReplyConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this reply?
blackboard.selectPost: Select a post to view details
blackboard.noWorkspacesDescription: Create or attach a workspace first, then the central blackboard will aggregate its tasks, discussions, and topology.
blackboard.objectivesSubtitle: Track objectives and keep execution pressure visible to the whole workspace.
blackboard.noObjectives: No objectives yet. The board still tracks delivery through tasks and discussion.
blackboard.tasksSubtitle: Turn shared context into execution. Create tasks from the board and move them as work progresses.
blackboard.taskPlaceholder: Create a task for the board...
blackboard.collaborationHint: Keep the workspace-wide collaboration stream inside the central blackboard so execution and discussion stay in one place.
blackboard.filesUnavailableTitle: Shared files are not wired here yet
blackboard.filesUnavailableBody: The central blackboard already combines discussion, goals, and execution. File operations can be added later when a workspace-scoped file endpoint is available.
blackboard.noAgents: No agents have been bound to this workspace yet.
blackboard.noNotes: No shared notes yet. Add workspace description, objectives, or pinned discussions to make this tab more useful.

arrangement.moveMode: Move mode: click a free hex
arrangement.description: Place AI employees, human seats, and corridor nodes on a shared command grid, then jump into the central blackboard whenever coordination needs more depth.
arrangement.canvasSubtitle: Select a hex to stage a new seat, update an agent, or drill into the blackboard.
arrangement.emptySelectionHint: Start by selecting an empty hex, an AI employee, or the center board.
arrangement.moveHint: Move mode is active. Choose a free hex or press Esc to cancel.
arrangement.staticHint: The center hex always opens the shared blackboard. Workstation edits stay on this surface.
arrangement.drawerSubtitle: Selection-aware actions appear here so the grid stays focused.

actions.addAgentHint: Bind an agent definition directly onto this hex.
actions.addCorridorHint: Reserve this slot for coordination or routing structure.
actions.addHumanSeatHint: Mark a human-operated slot for collaboration or review.

arrangement.drawerEmpty: Use the grid to stage a layout. The action drawer adapts to the selected workstation and keeps destructive actions away from the canvas.

context.blackboard: The central hex opens the full blackboard modal, where discussions, notes, and delivery state stay together.
context.empty: This hex is free. Use it to place a new agent, reserve a human seat, or carve a corridor into the command floor.
context.agent: Agents keep their own workspace binding id, so layout moves stay synced with the workspace roster and real-time events.
context.node: Topology nodes are persisted separately from agent bindings, which keeps human seats and corridor structure editable without disturbing execution bindings.
context.none: No hex selected yet. Pick a slot to inspect its available actions.
context.move: A move is armed. Select any free hex outside the center slot to complete it.
context.sync: Topology changes also stream back in real time. If another collaborator edits this workspace, the grid will reconcile from the event snapshot.

empty.title: No subagents yet

empty.noResultsHint: Try adjusting your search or filters.

modal.name: Name
modal.namePattern: Must start with lowercase letter, only a-z, 0-9, underscore
modal.systemPromptPlaceholder: Enter the system prompt for this SubAgent...
modal.triggerDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe when this SubAgent should be activated...
modal.allowedToolsTooltip: Comma-separated tool names, or * for all tools
modal.allowedSkillsTooltip: Comma-separated skill names this SubAgent can use
modal.allowedMcpServersTooltip: Comma-separated MCP server names this SubAgent can access
modal.spawnPolicyDescription: Configure how this SubAgent can spawn and be spawned by other agents.
modal.maxActiveRunsHint: Global cap on concurrent SubAgent runs
modal.allowedSubagentsPlaceholder: Select SubAgents that can spawn this one (empty = all)
modal.toolPolicyDescription: Configure which tools this SubAgent can use. Precedence determines how conflicts are resolved.
modal.denyFirstHint: Deny wins on conflict; unlisted tools are allowed.
modal.allowFirstHint: Allow wins on conflict; unlisted tools are allowed unless in deny.
modal.identityDescription: Configure the identity this SubAgent uses when spawning child agents. Leave empty to use defaults.
modal.identityDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe this SubAgent's role and personality when spawning child agents...

agentBindings.title: Agent Bindings

testRouting.title: Test Routing Resolution
testRouting.noMatch: No matching binding found for this context

modal.channelIdTooltip: Leave empty to match all channels of the selected type
modal.accountIdTooltip: Optional: bind to a specific user account
modal.peerIdTooltip: Optional: bind to a specific peer identity (highest specificity)
modal.groupIdTooltip: Optional: group bindings for broadcast routing (all agents in group receive messages)
modal.priorityTooltip: Higher priority bindings are matched first. Default is 0.
modal.specificityHint: Higher specificity = higher routing priority. The more fields filled, the higher the score.

agentDashboard.title: Agent Configuration
agentDashboard.description: Set the model, execution guardrails, tool boundaries, and runtime hooks that shape every Sisyphus turn in this tenant.
agentDashboard.scopeDescription: This page stays focused on live configuration surfaces. Definitions, skills, bindings, and plugins are linked as supporting systems instead of duplicated as mock dashboard widgets.
agentDashboard.noTenantTitle: Select a tenant to edit agent policy
agentDashboard.noTenantDescription: The configuration surface is scoped per tenant. Open it from a tenant route to review or edit the active runtime policy.
agentDashboard.feedbackTitle: Validate policy changes against live runs
agentDashboard.traceLoadErrorTitle: We could not load runtime traces
agentDashboard.traceLoadErrorDescription: Retry to fetch recent runs for this tenant before validating a policy change.
agentDashboard.traceChainLoadErrorDescription: The selected trace exists, but its detail chain could not be loaded right now.
agentDashboard.loadingTracesDescription: Fetching recent runs for this tenant to validate the current runtime policy.
agentDashboard.noTracesDescription: Start a conversation in Agent Workspace to confirm how the current model, tool policy, and runtime hooks behave in practice.
agentDashboard.editingModelApplies: Tenant policy applies broadly unless a narrower workspace binding overrides it.
agentDashboard.editingModelHooks: Runtime hooks are stored only when they diverge from catalog defaults.
agentDashboard.editingModelTraces: Use live traces to verify that a policy change actually changes behavior.

related.agentWorkspaceDescription: Run a conversation and inspect how the current runtime policy behaves.
related.skillsDescription: Manage reusable skill packs that the runtime policy can allow or restrict.
related.agentDefinitionsDescription: Update agent prompts, boundaries, and capabilities available to this tenant.
related.agentBindingsDescription: Choose where definitions are attached across projects and workspaces.
related.pluginsDescription: Review runtime plugins and the hook implementations backing this policy.
related.mcpServersDescription: Audit connected tool surfaces that affect agent execution at runtime.

agentConfigView.errorTitle: We couldn't load the tenant policy
agentConfigView.emptyDescription: The runtime returned an empty configuration payload. Reload to try again.
agentConfigView.title: Tenant runtime policy
agentConfigView.description: Every tenant conversation inherits this policy unless a narrower binding overrides it. Model selection, reasoning flags, tool boundaries, and runtime hook overrides all land here.
agentConfigView.defaultBanner: This tenant is inheriting the platform default. Save any change to create a custom policy for this tenant.

summary.temperatureHint: Lower values keep responses more deterministic
summary.toolPolicyHint: Explicit allow / deny rules stored for this tenant
summary.hookOverridesHint: Runtime hooks only appear here when this tenant diverges

modelReasoning.title: Model & reasoning
modelReasoning.description: How Sisyphus plans and reasons before it reaches for tools.
modelReasoning.temperatureHint: 0 is more deterministic, 2 is more exploratory.

executionGuardrails.title: Execution guardrails
executionGuardrails.description: Limits that shape runtime depth, tool execution, and the last persisted update.
executionGuardrails.maxWorkPlanStepsHint: Upper bound for the planner before it needs to simplify.
executionGuardrails.toolTimeoutHint: Default timeout applied to tool execution.

toolPolicy.title: Tool policy
toolPolicy.description: Explicit allow / deny lists. Empty lists mean the runtime stays on its default tool boundary.
toolPolicy.enabledToolsEmpty: No explicit allowlist. Runtime uses its default enabled tools.
toolPolicy.disabledToolsEmpty: No explicit denylist stored for this tenant.

runtimeHooks.title: Runtime hooks
runtimeHooks.description: Per-tenant runtime hook overrides. Hooks only appear here when the tenant diverges from the shared catalog defaults.
runtimeHooks.empty: No explicit hook overrides are stored. The tenant is currently inheriting the built-in runtime hook catalog defaults.
runtimeHooks.settingsHidden: Hook settings are visible to tenant admins only.

agentConfigEditor.title: Edit configuration
agentConfigEditor.description: Update the model, execution guardrails, tool policy, and runtime hook overrides applied across this tenant.
agentConfigEditor.saveErrorToast: We could not save the tenant policy. Try again.
agentConfigEditor.validationError: Some configuration values are invalid. Review the highlighted fields.
agentConfigEditor.accessDenied: You do not have access to edit this tenant policy.
agentConfigEditor.alertTitle: We couldn't save the tenant policy
agentConfigEditor.discardChangesDescription: You have unsaved edits in the tenant policy.

modelReasoning.title: Model & reasoning
modelReasoning.description: Set the primary model and the reasoning features that shape every response.
modelReasoning.modelIdentifierHint: Use the exact provider-prefixed model name the runtime should call.
modelReasoning.temperatureHint: Lower values keep responses more deterministic.
modelReasoning.patternLearningDescription: Let successful workflows teach the runtime reusable patterns.
modelReasoning.multiLevelThinkingDescription: Allow the planner to reason across both work plans and individual steps.

executionGuardrails.title: Execution guardrails
executionGuardrails.description: Set planning depth and execution timeouts for the tenant runtime.
executionGuardrails.maxWorkPlanStepsHint: Upper bound for the planner before it needs to simplify.
executionGuardrails.maxWorkPlanStepsRequired: Please set a max work plan step count.
executionGuardrails.toolTimeoutHint: Default timeout applied to tool execution.
executionGuardrails.toolTimeoutMin: Value must be at least 1 second.
executionGuardrails.multiAgentRoutingDescription: This is controlled at the system level through

toolPolicy.title: Tool policy
toolPolicy.description: Use explicit allow and deny lists when this tenant needs a narrower tool boundary than the shared runtime default.
toolPolicy.enabledToolsHint: Comma-separated internal tool names. Leave empty to inherit the runtime default.
toolPolicy.disabledToolsHint: Comma-separated internal tool names to explicitly deny for this tenant.

runtimeHooks.title: Runtime hooks
runtimeHooks.description: Only save a hook when this tenant needs to diverge from the shared catalog defaults.
runtimeHooks.catalogUnavailableDescription: Runtime hook catalog is currently unavailable. You can still update the rest of this tenant policy.
runtimeHooks.defaultHookDescription: This hook applies runtime reminders during execution.
runtimeHooks.noExtraSettings: This hook exposes no extra settings beyond enablement and priority.
runtimeHooks.empty: No runtime hooks are currently exposed for tenant customization.

custom.title: Custom hooks
custom.description: Add unmanaged hooks when you need a tenant-specific script or plugin executor that is not part of the shared catalog.
custom.empty: No custom hooks are configured for this tenant.
custom.identityDescription: Configure lifecycle, executor identity, and runtime settings separately. These fields are sent directly to the runtime hook config model.
custom.settingsHint: Use a JSON object only. Executor identity fields belong above, not inside settings.

validation.title: Custom hook is incomplete
validation.summary: Review the custom hook fields before saving.
validation.entrypoint: Entrypoint is required for script and plugin hooks.
validation.settingsObject: Settings must be a JSON object.

cards.synthesizeReportsPrompt: Help me synthesize a report from my recent findings. Create a clear executive summary.

undo.title: Undo Action
undo.description: This will ask the agent to undo the action. The agent will attempt to revert any changes made.

templates.title: Prompt Templates
templates.analyzeCodebasePrompt: Analyze the codebase structure and provide a high-level overview including architecture patterns, key dependencies, and areas for improvement.
templates.findBugsPrompt: Search for potential bugs, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues in the project. Focus on critical issues first.
templates.performanceAuditPrompt: Analyze the application for performance bottlenecks including database queries, API response times, memory usage, and frontend rendering.
templates.writeTestsPrompt: Write comprehensive unit tests for the most critical modules. Aim for 80%+ coverage with meaningful test cases.
templates.refactorCodePrompt: Identify and refactor code that violates DRY, SOLID principles, or has high complexity. Propose cleaner alternatives.
templates.addFeaturePrompt: I want to add a new feature: [describe feature]. Plan the implementation, identify files to modify, and implement it step by step.
templates.fixErrorPrompt: I'm getting this error: [paste error]. Diagnose the root cause and fix it.
templates.writeDocsPrompt: Generate comprehensive documentation for the project including API reference, setup guide, and architecture overview.
templates.writeReadmePrompt: Create or improve the project README with sections for: overview, quick start, installation, configuration, usage examples, and contributing.
templates.explainCodePrompt: Explain how the core system works, walking through the main execution flow from entry point to key outputs.
templates.brainstormPrompt: Help me brainstorm ideas for improving this project. Consider UX improvements, new features, technical debt reduction, and scalability.
templates.marketplaceDesc: Browse and install SubAgent templates to extend your agent capabilities.

onboarding.welcomeDesc: Your AI-powered workspace for analysis, coding, and automation.
onboarding.inputDesc: Type messages naturally, or use / to access commands and tools.
onboarding.layoutDesc: Switch between Chat, Code, Desktop, Focus, and Canvas modes with Cmd+1-5.
onboarding.templateDesc: Browse pre-built prompts for common tasks.
onboarding.searchDesc: Use Cmd+F to search within any conversation.

theme.highContrastDescription: Enhanced visibility with stronger colors and borders

none.no_preset_answer: No preset options - enter your answer below
none.no_preset_decision: No preset options - enter your decision below

multiselect.limit: You may select up to {{max}} items

user_profile.title: Personal Profile
user_profile.subtitle: Manage your account settings and preferences.

basic.title: Basic Information
basic.about_hint: Brief description for your profile. Max 200 characters.

toast.success_desc: Your profile information has been updated.

expired.title: Invitation Expired
expired.description: This invitation has expired. Please contact the workspace admin for a new invitation.

invalid.title: Invalid Invitation
invalid.description: This invitation link is invalid or has already been used.

error.title: Error
error.description: An error occurred while processing your invitation.

accepted.title: Welcome!
accepted.description: You have successfully joined the workspace.

login.title: Accept Your Invitation
login.prompt: Please log in to accept this invitation

emailMismatch.title: Email Mismatch
emailMismatch.description: This invitation was sent to {{invitedEmail}}, but you are logged in as {{currentEmail}}. You can still accept the invitation.

members.title: Members
members.description: Manage who has access to this workspace.
members.removeConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this member?

dangerZone.title: Danger Zone
dangerZone.deleteDescription: Once deleted, all data in this workspace will be permanently removed.
dangerZone.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this workspace? This action cannot be undone.

workspaceDetail.title: Workspace
workspaceDetail.errorLoadingWorkspace: Failed to load workspace. Please try again.

chat.noMessages: No messages yet. Start the conversation!

forceChangePassword.title: Change Your Password
forceChangePassword.subtitle: Your administrator requires you to change your password before continuing.
forceChangePassword.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters.

deadLetterQueue.title: Dead Letter Queue
deadLetterQueue.description: Manage failed messages and handle exceptions

errors.reasonRequired: Please provide a reason for discarding

confirm.cleanupExpiredDesc: This will permanently delete all expired messages. This action cannot be undone.
confirm.cleanupResolvedDesc: This will permanently delete all resolved messages. This action cannot be undone.

discard.title: Discard Message(s)
discard.confirmMessage: You are about to discard {{count}} message(s). This action cannot be undone.
discard.reasonLabel: Please provide a reason for discarding:
discard.reasonPlaceholder: e.g., Duplicate event, Stale data, Manual fix applied...

system.featureDisabled: This feature is not available in your current edition

org.setupRequiredDescription: You need to configure your organization before accessing other features

gene.deleteReviewConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this review?
gene.reviewPlaceholder: Share your thoughts about this gene...
gene.noReviews: No reviews yet. Be the first to review!

webhooks.name: Name
webhooks.eventsRequired: Please select at least one event
webhooks.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this webhook?

device.title: Connect a device
device.subtitle: Enter the 8-character code shown in your terminal to grant access.
device.invalidCode: Enter the 8-character code shown in your terminal.
device.approvedSubtitle: Your terminal should be signed in. You can close this tab.
device.signInSubtitle: You must be signed in to approve a CLI login. After signing in, you will be returned here automatically.
device.footer: Only approve codes you just generated yourself. A 30-day API key will be issued to the waiting CLI.
